case_title: JAYESH PATIL VS. DIRECTOR OF THE DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: DECLARATION OF PERSONAL SERVICE OF (1) DMV'S OPPOS TO PETITIONER'S EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR A STAY & (2) PRELIMINARY MEMO OF P&A OPPOSING PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE FILED BY RESPONDENT DIRECTOR OF MOTOR VEHICLES

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER JAYESH PATIL'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. THIS COURT DOES NOT HAVE JURISDICTION TO REVIEW HEARING OFFICER ROBLES' DENIAL OF PETITIONER'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE BECAUSE HIS APPLICATION FOR REVIEW WAS NOT TIMELY PER GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 11524. OFFICER SYSUM'S UNSIGNED REPORTS ARE ADMISSIBLE. (MACDONALD V. GUTIERREZ (2004) 32 CAL.4TH 150, 159.) RESPONDENT ESTABLISHED THAT PETITIONER WAS LAWFULLY ARRESTED AND PROPERLY ADMONISHED. MR. HARKNESS PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing And Notice Of Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Aug-15-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert Andrew Harkness, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 510.622.2216, for Respondent and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER JAYESH PATIL's petition for Writ Of Mandate is Denied. This court does not have jurisdiction to review Hearing Officer Robles' denial of Petitioner's request for a continuance because his application for review was not timely per Government Code section 11524. Officer Sysum's unsigned reports are admissible. (MacDonald v. Gutierrez (2004) 32 Cal.4th 150, 159.) Respondent established that Petitioner was lawfully arrested and properly admonished. Mr. Harkness prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976
